items.title: Major disturbance unfolds at Perth's maximum security prison

children.content: A prison officer has been injured in a major disturbance that caused significant damage at Perth’s maximum-security prison.

items.title: Parents want better housing for teachers in remote WA town

image.altText: Munglinup to lose a school bus service

children.content: A remote school 500km south-east of Perth is struggling to retain staff as housing pressures force incoming teachers to live far from work.

items.title: Stink bomb attack planners taken to court by energy giant Woodside

children.content: Energy giant Woodside has taken climate activists to court in a bid to unmask the conspirators behind a stink-bomb protest that forced the evacuation of its Perth headquarters.

image.altText: Kristen and Joana stand side by side outside court while looking straight at the camera

items.title: WA home ownership rate grows as Sydney hits 70-year low

children.content: Sydney is no longer affordable for most, as new data reveals the number of people who own the home they live in is the lowest it has been in decades.

image.altText: Generic apartments.

items.title: Carnival atmosphere as BHP staff down tools for landmark strike

children.content: Workers at the mining giant's operations in Port Hedland have walked off the job in one of the most significant industrial disputes to hit the resources sector in a quarter of a century.

articles.title: BHP hits production record prior to landmark industrial action

children.content: As hundreds of BHP workers prepare to down tools in a landmark strike, the miner has revealed its highest-ever levels of iron ore production.

image.altText: An image of iron ore operations including loaders and conveyer belts

BHP staff walk off the job in Port Hedland for an eight-hour strike.

items.title: Ex-prisoners say Australia should do more for citizens detained overseas

children.content: Australians who have endured prolonged detention overseas call on the government to adopt a more strategic approach to cases involving citizens, especially those held by countries that practice "hostage diplomacy".

gallery.altText: A man in a white singlet takes a selfie with a smiling woman with her head on his shoulder.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a white singlet takes a selfie with a smiling woman with her head on his shoulder.

items.title: Former diplomat complains of Triple Zero outages on Telstra's 'priority' service

children.content: Telstra promised Alastair Gaisford that his wife's healthcare was a priority. But he says 15 landline outages, including some that could have prevented the ability to call Triple Zero in an emergency, have convinced him otherwise.

image.altText: A man with short grey hair sits in a living room with a box labelled "Telstra Files" sitting on his lap.

items.title: Sam Neill died from a common infection. Here's how it turned deadly

children.content: After the actor's death from pneumonia, here's what to know about the common infection, its symptoms and why it can sometimes become deadly.

image.altText: A close up of Sam Neill with tears in his eyes

items.title: Scars of alleged Invasion Day terror attack run deep six months on

children.content: A day of evidence reflecting on racism, hate and violence directed towards Aboriginal people highlights the ongoing impact of what police described as a potential "mass-casualty event" at a Perth rally.

items.title: Vaping on decline as young Australians shift to other nicotine products

children.content: Vaping and smoking rates are on the decline, but a shift to other nicotine products could represent an increase in average use.

image.altText: A hand holding a purple vape, Bunbury tower in the background

items.title: 'Best place to look for life': Atmosphere discovered on Earth-like planet

children.content: The search for life beyond Earth has marked a new discovery — evidence of an atmosphere on an Earth-like planet beyond our Solar System with modelling suggesting it may be a water world.

image.altText: Artists impression of LHS 1140 b orbiting its star.

items.title: Win for MS patients facing drug pricing cliff

children.content: Health Minister Mark Butler has accepted independent advice for a rapid review of MS drugs to ensure they remain available on the PBS.

image.altText: A pile of drugs on a table, all are different shapes and sizes.

items.title: Albanese announces AI plan, Hanson joins Rinehart in Sicily

children.content: It might not have been a sitting week but it has been a busy few days in politics with the prime minister's AI announcement, a raft of politicians inking book deals and a spotting of Pauline Hanson and Gina Rinehart in Sicily.

items.title: Seven-kilometre artwork creates colour journey through Perth suburbs

children.content: Under the raised railway that runs through Perth's south-east is a public art project that took five months to complete and required 2,000 litres of paint.

items.title: 'People want something different': Ardern on the rise of fringe politics

children.content: The former New Zealand prime minister reflects on leading through crisis, becoming a mother while running a country and why she believes voters are craving something different.

image.altText: Jacinda Ardern speaks at a podium. Microphones are pointed at her and men in suits are behind her.

items.title: AusAlerts could compromise 'safe phones' for women at DV risk

children.content: Regional women experiencing domestic violence are at high risk of their safe phones being discovered during tests of the new AusAlert disaster warning system.

image.altText: A phone notification with the words 'Priority AusAlert'.

items.title: Friday news quiz: The iconic film Sam Neill rejected, a royal reunion and a mystery outbreak

children.content: Our weekly quiz has the film Sam Neill "didn't think was funny", plus the sweetness at the centre of our galaxy.

items.title: This discovery in a Tokyo bookshop made me run away to the mountains

children.content: In a rare bookstore in Japan, I find a manga written by a convicted cannibal. It becomes the subject of a moral dilemma I've been grappling with as a filmmaker.

image.altText: Two hands hold a book open. The pages feature an illustration of a face.

items.title: How a compulsive list maker ended up making 'extraordinary' observations

children.content: Mike Connor visited the same park for 40 years and wrote down what he saw. He didn't realise he was incidentally chronicling a changing environment and contributing to science.

children.content: Labor insiders expect ongoing debate about artificial intelligence when party faithful gather to hash out a policy platform to guide the next three years.

items.title: 'No white flag' in by-election race with One Nation, Liberal leader says

children.content: WA's Liberal leader says "there is no white flag being waved" in the upcoming Secret Harbour by-election, but states any result other than Labor retaining the seat, namely a win by One Nation, will be a "victory" for the party.
